##NAMES=Marsturite
##RRUFFID=R141210
##IDEAL CHEMISTRY=NaCaMn^2+^_3_Si_5_O_14_(OH)
##LOCALITY=Wessels mine, Kalahari Manganese Field, Northern Cape Province, South Africa
##OWNER=RRUFF
##SOURCE=Jaco and Tania Janse van Nieuwenhuizen
##DESCRIPTION=Colorless granular crystals in a vein. The matrix is a mixture of quartz and bixbyite, associated with schizolite
##STATUS=The identification of this mineral is confirmed by single-crystal X-ray diffraction and chemical analysis
##URL=https://www.rruff.net/odr/rruff_sample/R141210
##MEASURED CHEMISTRY=(Na_0.80_Ca_0.13_)Ca_1.00_(Mn^2+^_2.81_Mg_0.12_Fe^2+^_0.01_)Si_5.00_O_14_(OH)
##DIFFRACTION SAMPLE DESCRIPTION=Single crystal, powder profile is calculated
##CELL PARAMETERS=a: 7.6882 b: 11.701 c: 6.7768 alpha: 92.242 beta: 94.363 gamma: 106.744 volume: 580.9 crystal system: triclinic
##FILETYPE=Raman Processed
##RAMAN WAVELENGTH=780
